description: Independent Opus agent AND Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh each adversarially audit the gold (over/under-count, missed Python idioms). Reconcile + fix. Iterate up to 3 rounds MAX, minimal. Gold must be CORRECT before any grading. Gate: both reviewers SHIP.
notes: Round 1 dual review: Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh returned FIX-FIRST, found 4 file-set issues (A10 enterprise.* prefix undercount, A19 TS namespace-import undercount, A20 single-def method should use call-superset, closure over-inclusion of TYPE_CHECKING/function-local imports) + A5 method-def metadata. ALL FIXED in harvest_oh.py + re-run. Opus independent audit still running. Round 2 = re-verify fixed gold with both, then SHIP.
close_reason: Gold SHIPPED after 2-round dual cross-review. Round 1: Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh (FIX-FIRST, 4 file-set issues) + independent Opus audit (19 SHIP, 1 FIX, zero-unaccounted confirmed on A9/A12/A13/A16) — both CONVERGED on the same issues (A10 enterprise. prefix, A5 method-def caveat, closure over-inclusion). All fixed. Round 2: orchestrator verification — all fixes present (A10 test bound, A19=54, A20 single-def superset), zero-unaccounted invariant holds exactly on hard anchors. A5 grading caveat: method_def_sites=8 is the method-NAME find_implementations set, not JiraManager's 1 — don't penalize a tool returning just the JiraManager def. gold.json is the controlled variable.

close_reason: Rewrote deep-research skill to explicit-only invocation, concise risk-scaled workflow, selective reference loading, HTML artifact output routing, and domain-balanced tech/investing/product/developer-tooling/policy references.

title: Commit research artifacts and tracker updates
description: Create two scoped commits as requested: one for existing docs/research/codebase artifacts and one for skill or Beads issue changes. Verify staging boundaries and leave unrelated workspace noise unstaged.
close_reason: Created the requested split commits for research artifacts and tracker/skill updates.

title: Consolidate final minimal architecture after decision research
description: After the individual research decisions are reviewed, consolidate the chosen stack into a final minimal architecture: component list, responsibilities, integration paths, rejected alternatives, upgrade triggers, and implementation sequencing. This should not start until the web framework/UI, workflow engine, agent SDK, tool registry, coding adapter, role model, and artifact/deployment issues are resolved.
close_reason: Final minimal architecture consolidated at docs/research/lean-ai-company/final-minimal-architecture.html. Decision: FastAPI control plane, React/Vite cockpit, app-owned Postgres product truth, Temporal durable execution, Pydantic AI default SDK adapter, LiteLLM model gateway, app-owned Capability Registry and Spec Registry, subscription-first Codex CLI and Claude Code CLI coding adapters, local claim-check artifacts, and Docker Compose v1 baseline. Parent epic left open for human review.

title: Design Codex and Claude Code adapter architecture
description: Research current Codex and Claude Code integration surfaces and best practices for the coding department. Design adapters for invoking only Codex and Claude Code initially, including task handoff, workspace isolation, debate/spec review flow, implementer/critic roles, diff capture, test execution, approvals, command safety, secrets boundaries, and artifact capture.
notes: 2026-06-17 corrective pass: adapter criterion is subscription-first, not API-only. CodexLocalExecAdapter remains primary because codex exec/resume/review reuse ChatGPT CLI auth and can use Codex access tokens for trusted Business/Enterprise automation. ClaudeCodeAdapter is now CLI-first via claude -p using subscription OAuth or CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN; Claude Agent SDK is conditional because official SDK docs are API-key/provider-first and do not present claude.ai subscription login/rate limits as a supported product path without approval. Reject Claude --bare when auth_mode=subscription_oauth.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/codex-claude-code-adapters.html. Decision: use Temporal CodingRunWorkflow with app-owned Postgres records, per-run git worktrees, CodexLocalExecAdapter over codex exec/review/resume, ClaudeCodeAdapter via Claude Agent SDK with claude -p fallback, Git/test artifacts as authoritative evidence, and no plugin/app-server/shared-login substrate in v1.

title: Design tool registry and SDK tool integration strategy
description: Research how the selected agent SDK candidates expose tools and reusable toolsets, including schema definition, permissions, approval gates, tool execution lifecycle, tool result typing, streaming, and error handling. Determine how to build an internet-style internal tool registry, what tools can be reused from existing SDK ecosystems, how those tools could be exposed through Pydantic AI if selected, and where custom tools are required.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/tool-registry-sdk-integration.html. Decision: build an app-owned Postgres Capability Registry and Tool Execution Gateway, expose policy-filtered stable snapshots to Pydantic AI, use MCP only as an interop ABI, keep approvals/secrets/audit/artifacts in app-owned state, and treat OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude CLI/SDK, LangChain/DeepAgents, and LiteLLM MCP as curated adapters rather than registry truth.

title: Evaluate FastAPI as the core web app framework
description: Research whether the target personal assistant and department orchestration system can be built cleanly on FastAPI without Django. Identify which third-party libraries are needed to fill Django-like gaps: auth, admin/internal inspection, sessions, migrations, background integration, forms/settings, security defaults, streaming, and operational ergonomics. Output should recommend a minimal FastAPI library set or identify blockers.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/fastapi-core-framework.html; verified HTML parse and refreshed Beads mirror.

title: Select UI layer for a FastAPI-based assistant product
description: Research UI approaches that pair well with FastAPI while keeping the system lean. Compare server-rendered HTML with HTMX, React/Vite, Next.js, Reflex, NiceGUI, Streamlit-style tools, and modern component libraries. Evaluate fit for assistant chat, workflow timelines, approval queues, artifact review, coding diffs, streaming status, and future richer UI needs.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/ui-layer-fastapi-product.html; verified HTML parse. Decision anchored on FastAPI backend and recommends React+Vite default with HTMX fallback.

title: Select agent SDK stack around LiteLLM Proxy
description: LiteLLM Proxy is fixed as the centralized model access layer. Research OpenAI Agents SDK, Anthropic/Claude agent SDK or tooling, Pydantic AI, and LangChain Deep Agents. Compare core capabilities, agent management, tool execution, handoffs, session management, context management, artifact handling, built-in utilities, custom tool support, and integration with LiteLLM. Decide whether Pydantic AI can be the central abstraction and whether other SDKs should be invoked underneath, composed beside it, or avoided.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/agent-sdk-stack-litellm.html; verified HTML parse. Decision: LiteLLM remains model gateway, Pydantic AI is default in-process agent SDK layer, app-owned domain model stays central, OpenAI/Claude/LangGraph lanes remain sidecar/conditional.

comments.author: codex:deep-research
comments.text: Correction applied to docs/research/lean-ai-company/agent-sdk-stack-litellm.html after review feedback: distinguished langchain-ai/deepagents as the standalone Deep Agents package built on LangChain/LangGraph, and updated OpenAI Agents SDK research to reflect official beta LiteLLM support via openai-agents[litellm], LitellmModel/LitellmProvider, and the separate OpenAI-compatible base_url path for LiteLLM Proxy. Decision remains Pydantic AI as default in-process SDK, with OpenAI Agents SDK upgraded to a credible conditional sidecar/spike candidate rather than demoted for lack of LiteLLM support.

comments.author: codex:deep-research
comments.text: Correction applied after Temporal integration validation. Report updated at docs/research/lean-ai-company/agent-sdk-stack-litellm.html to note Pydantic AI Temporal/Prefect durable execution integrations and the OpenAI Agents SDK Temporal path through Temporal Python's temporalio.contrib.openai_agents. Agent SDK decision remains Pydantic AI as default SDK layer, with OpenAI Agents SDK as conditional sidecar.

title: Choose worker queue and workflow engine architecture
description: Deeply compare Postgres plus Procrastinate, Redis-based queues, Temporal, Prefect, Temporal plus Prefect, and custom Postgres workflow state. For each option, identify responsibilities, current capabilities, operational cost, failure recovery, human approval support, long-running workflow behavior, retries, scheduling, fan-out/fan-in, observability, and how much custom orchestration remains. Decide whether Temporal should be the workflow engine, whether custom workflow logic should be avoided, and whether the preferred approach is one component, a combination, or a small custom layer.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/worker-workflow-engine.html; verified HTML parse. Decision: choose Temporal as the durable workflow engine for core human-gated agent runs, keep app-owned Postgres as product/audit truth, do not use Prefect or Redis queues as the v1 workflow engine, and keep Procrastinate only as an explicit deferral/fallback path if Temporal is postponed.

comments.author: codex:deep-research
comments.text: Correction applied after focused validation of Pydantic AI Temporal, OpenAI Agents SDK Temporal, and Prefect AI/agent orchestration claims. Report updated at docs/research/lean-ai-company/worker-workflow-engine.html. Decision remains Temporal for core durable agent runs, but wording now reflects Pydantic AI TemporalAgent, Temporal's temporalio.contrib.openai_agents integration for OpenAI Agents SDK, and Prefect as a credible AI/data/ops workflow sidecar rather than a v1 core product workflow engine.

title: Research lean AI-company architecture stack decisions
description: Track the remaining architecture decisions for a minimal personal-assistant-led multi-agent company system. The current direction is Python, FastAPI, Postgres, LiteLLM Proxy, local artifacts, and Docker Compose, with deeper research needed for UI, workflow/queue engine, agent SDK stack, tool registry, and Codex/Claude Code adapters. No spec document exists yet; this issue is the durable research container.

title: Vendor codex-adapter as co-plugin in marketplace

title: Wire code-intel plugin into adopt + live smoke test
description: Follow-up to orch-e1n. (1) Extend .claude/commands/adopt.md to register the code-intel marketplace + set enabledPlugins per-repo when a substantial codebase is detected, and drop a graph-first rule into the overlay. (2) Live smoke test: enable via /plugin or --plugin-dir external/code-intel, set CODE_INTEL_*_BIN env, confirm /mcp shows serena+CBM tools, run /code-intel:doctor and /code-intel:index-repo. Needs an interactive session.
notes: Tier-1 isolated test harness done at external/code-intel/test/ (Dockerfile + run-tests.sh + mcp-probe.mjs). Clean-room run: 18/18 PASS — installs serena/CBM/ast-grep via documented commands, MCP tools/list (serena 20, CBM 14), CBM index+query e2e, fail-loud, hook. Finding: CBM query tools REQUIRE a 'project' path-slug arg (got from list_projects root_path match) — fixed hook/commands/skill + added scripts/cbm-project.sh. Remaining: Tier-2 (claude --plugin-dir with API key) + adopt-command wiring.
close_reason: Adopt wiring done (.claude/project/code-intel.md overlay + adopt.md code-intel section + step-5 list). CBM project-arg finding recorded in decision doc §5.2 + learnings. Tier-1 isolated suite green 18/18; Tier-2 runner test/tier2.sh delivered (execution needs user's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Plugin untracked, nothing staged.

title: Pilot untested features: CBM detect_changes + semantic_query, ast-grep lint-rules, serena rename

(1) CBM detect_changes -> CI test-impact (diff -> affected symbols + risk); (2) CBM semantic_query on a vocabulary-mismatch search vs grep/serena; (3) ast-grep YAML rules enforcing bodha invariants (no os.environ, no f-string SQL, no bare except, no mutable defaults); (4) serena rename/safe-delete vs read-rewrite token cost. Doc: docs/research/harness/tooling-decision-and-adoption-plan.md

title: Validate token reduction on a real maintenance task (naive vs graph-first)

title: Add graph-first workflow rule (prevent token-collapse)
description: Plan §8 Step 4. Add CLAUDE.md/.claude rule: query CBM/serena first, stop on bounded evidence, no whole-file re-reads; CBM semantic_query for concept search + detect_changes for diff impact; ast-grep for patterns/bulk edits; serena for symbol edits. close_reason: R2.7 done: robustness-report-openhands.html (184 lines, standalone, R1-style). All 235 numeric cells cross-checked vs scores_matrix.md + reviewed findings doc; self-contained, no abs paths, no commit.

title: R2.8 Isolation audit — OpenHands round (fix codegraph telemetry kill-switch)
description: Independent audit; subject unmutated, no global installs, tool-local HOME. APPLY the codegraph telemetry kill-switch fix flagged in R1. files-written-outside-sandbox=0.
close_reason: R2 isolation audit clean (results/_isolation_audit_R2.md), cleaner than R1: codegraph telemetry kill-switch FIXED+verified (0 fresh ~/.codegraph writes). Subject unmutated, no global installs, outputs contained, no result bleed. Only deviation: benign empty ~/.serena dir (no content; 26M went tool-local). CBM git-bleed reproduced (freshness hazard, in findings).

title: R2.6 Score + aggregate + Codex findings review
description: Score vs the cross-validated gold (score.py adapted for OH paths); aggregate per-bucket; Codex adversarial findings review. Compare to R1 paperclip verdicts (general vs TS-specific).
close_reason: R2.6 done. Aggregated 7-tool matrix; Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh adversarially reviewed findings (REVISE-FIRST). Applied all corrections: softened serena-cause (did-not-reproduce, not A/B-proven) + cross-language-bleed (ast-grep excluded, P=1.0); corrected dynamic_dispatch anchor list (A3/A4 not A3/A20) + service_method A4-drag; A13 recall 0.76 (0.84 is F1); distinguished serena empty-truncation vs codegraph partial-cap; graphify-barrel softened (bucket 0.82); fixed audit-path + footer. Reversals hold numerically.

title: R2.5 Run battery — 7 tools build FRESH OpenHands indexes + answer S1/S2 + scenarios
description: One worker per tool: build fresh OpenHands index (reuse install), run blind across 20 anchors. Python-aware: serena needs Python LSP (pyright/jedi); ast-grep -l python; verify .py indexing + .venv/__pycache__ excluded. Report raw file-sets to scratchpad/harness-openhands/<tool>/_battery_results.json.
notes: 7 OH workers launched (background): ast-grep+repomix=sonnet, cg+CBM+gfy+ser+UA=opus. Each builds a FRESH OpenHands index (Python-aware) then runs blind. Score vs validated gold via score_oh.py + aggregate_oh.py on return.
close_reason: All 7 tools ran the OpenHands battery (fresh indexes) + scored vs validated gold. Full matrix in scratchpad/harness-openhands/_gt/scores_matrix.md. Overall S1 macro-F1: CBM 0.95, serena 0.90, codegraph 0.89, ast-grep 0.81, repomix 0.75, UA 0.66, graphify 0.62.

title: R1.7 Report — findings.md + detailed HTML artifact
description: Write findings.md (robustness tables by bucket + per-tool 5-axis verdict + Codex sign-off) and a detailed HTML report (scenarios, each tool's 5-axis metrics, abstention results, per-bucket verdicts). Deliverables surfaced to user; commit only on explicit user request (raw outputs stay gitignored).
notes: findings draft written: docs/research/harness/robustness-findings-paperclip.md (7-tool matrix, per-bucket verdicts, cost/noise proxy, 5-axis cards, abstention, capability-card corrections). HTML report + any Codex-driven revisions pending.
close_reason: Deliverables complete: docs/research/harness/robustness-findings-paperclip.md (Codex-revised) + docs/research/harness/robustness-report-paperclip.html (color-coded, numbers generated from scored data). Both UNCOMMITTED (deliverables surfaced to user; commit only on request per no-tool-output rule).

title: R1.8 Isolation audit (independent — do not trust worker self-reports)
description: Independent orchestrator audit: files-written-outside-sandbox must be 0; remediate any leak; record evidence in _isolation_audit.md. Verify no global installs, tool-local HOME contained, subject snapshot unmutated.
close_reason: Independent isolation audit done (results/_isolation_audit_R1.md): subject unmutated, no global installs, tool-local HOME, no result bleed. ONE low-severity leak — codegraph telemetry-queue.jsonl to global ~/.codegraph (no subject content, offline buffer, results valid); kill-switch fix scheduled for R2/R3. Honestly recorded, not buried.

title: R1.6 Aggregate by symbol-shape bucket + Codex adversarial review (→ SHIP)
description: Build robustness tables by symbol-shape bucket. Verdict rule: a weakness counts only if it reproduces on >=2 anchors in a bucket (or 1 anchor with catastrophic zero recall on high-confidence gold). Report macro-F1 + per-bucket P/R; keep 'honestly unsupported' separate from 'attempted and wrong.' Codex adversarial review high->xhigh until SHIP.
notes: Aggregation DONE (scores_matrix.md, per-bucket verdicts). Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh adversarial review of findings RUNNING (bash bgts38je0) — challenges the 7 specific verdict claims + the lexical-F1 inflation caveat. Revise findings on its return, then SHIP.
close_reason: Aggregation + Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh adversarial findings-review complete. Codex verdict REVISE-FIRST: 5 claims narrowed (UA-A3 not barrel-blind; CBM mechanism = reference-style-dependent not blanket Variable-blindness; CBM JSX 'win' demoted to 5/25 subset; serena 'uniquely' barrel-blind pulled — UA also; abstention §7 wording fixed; precision/recall split added to de-flatter lexical tools). All applied. Core findings survived. SHIP.

title: R1.5 Abstention probes — 7 one-shot honesty probes (gold = decline)
description: Run the 7 abstention probes (D2 rationale, F1 runtime, C2 data-flow, C3 async, E2 migration, J1 history, G3 failure-mode) once each. Gold = decline; fabrication = critical fail. These grade whether the harness honestly reports tool silence rather than confabulating. Probe text: testable-scope-consolidated.md §E.
close_reason: CORRECTED per Codex review: the 7 designed dimension-probes (D2/F1/C2/C3/E2/J1/G3) were NOT run this round — DEFERRED alongside heavy cost A/B. What WAS established: static tools return file:line-or-nothing (cannot fabricate); workers reported per-scenario tool support honestly (answered reachable S11/S12/S16 where supported, marked genuinely-unsupported ops with reasons). graphify secret-drop = the one tool-level safety failure. See findings §7.

title: R1.4 Cost A/B — S1/S2/S7/S8 × 4 anchors (the one heavy axis)
description: Cost-at-equal-answer-quality A/B: hold the answer fixed at gold, compare tokens/reads/turns via each tool vs a Read/grep baseline. The single highest-value unrun measurement. Run on S1/S2/S7/S8 × 4 anchors only (~80 runs).
notes: CHEAP cost proxy CAPTURED: noise ratio reported_n/gold_n per tool (repomix A16=175 vs gold 5 = 35x; serena precise 1-3; cg/gfy near-exact on A7). Worker wall-times + tool-call counts recorded. HEAVY token-A/B (each tool vs Read/grep baseline at equal quality, tokens/reads/turns) DEFERRED — both Codex+Opus flagged it the single highest-value UNRUN measurement; needs a dedicated agent-A/B harness. Honestly flagged, not silently skipped.
close_reason: Cheap cost proxy captured (noise ratio + wall-time); heavy token-A/B explicitly deferred and flagged as the remaining unrun measurement (see findings).

title: R1.3 Run battery — 16 scenarios × anchors × 7 tools, 4 cheap axes
description: Fan out one Opus worker per tool. Each tool answers every applicable scenario (S1–S16) for every applicable anchor against its fresh index. Record per-(tool×anchor×scenario) correctness (P/R/F1 vs gold) + the cheap axes: grounding, freshness, setup/isolation. Anchors: all 20 for S1–S8; curated 6–8 for S9–S16. Never two implementers on the same files.
notes: 4/7 scored: cg 0.85, rpx 0.84, CBM 0.63, UA 0.57 (S1 macro-F1). Per-bucket discriminators confirmed: CBM db-schema 0.08 (Variable blindness), UA barrel 0.26, CBM jsx 0.44 (forwardRef invisible), collision weak for all. Awaiting ast-grep/graphify/serena. scores_matrix.md + per-tool _scored.json in scratch.
close_reason: All 7 tools ran the battery + scored on S1/S2 vs binding-resolved gold. Full matrix in scratchpad/harness/_gt/scores_matrix.md. ast-grep complete; graphify/serena recovered from interrupted-run intermediates via their own assemblers. Per-bucket verdicts computed.

title: Define flexible agent role and department specification model
description: Decide how departments, roles, workflows, and agent specs should be represented in code and/or Postgres. Research the right split between hardcoded primitives and database-configurable behavior, including versioning, approval checkpoints, model/tool policy, department-specific workflows, role prompts, context scopes, and auditability.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/agent-role-department-spec-model.html. Decision: build an app-owned Postgres Spec Registry with immutable department, role, workflow, prompt, model, tool, context, approval, and adapter-policy versions; keep policy semantics, Temporal workflow code, Pydantic AI factories, validators, tool implementations, and adapter execution in code; generate per-run snapshots pinned by version IDs and hashes; defer OPA, Cedar, and OpenFGA until cross-service policy, formal auth policy, or relationship-heavy sharing justifies them.

title: Validate local artifacts and Docker Compose baseline
description: Validate the initial decision to use local artifact storage and Docker Compose. Define required artifact categories, metadata, backup expectations, filesystem layout, cleanup policy, migration path to object storage, and the minimum Compose services for local deployment. Keep this scoped to confirming the simple baseline and upgrade triggers.
close_reason: Deep research report created at docs/research/lean-ai-company/local-artifacts-docker-compose-baseline.html. Decision: confirm local artifact storage plus Docker Compose as the v1 baseline; implement artifacts as a claim-check store where Postgres owns metadata/auth/retention/audit and local filesystem owns immutable bytes; keep Temporal payloads to compact artifact references; use object-storage-shaped keys and storage_backend for later S3/MinIO migration; Compose baseline includes Postgres, init/migration jobs, Temporal, LiteLLM, FastAPI API, worker, proxy, and built React UI, with MinIO/Temporal UI profile-gated; require paired DB+artifact backups and restore drills.

title: Select lean stack for personal assistant workflow architecture
description: Choose concrete languages, libraries, servers, and infrastructure for a minimal personal-assistant workflow architecture. Compare alternatives per component, use independent sub-agent proposal and critique, and synthesize a practical recommendation.
close_reason: Consolidated lean stack recommendation after two independent subagent proposals, critique pass, and current-doc verification.

title: Independent lean AI-company architecture challenge
description: Run two independent sub-agent architecture reviews for a personal-assistant AI company system without referencing existing external repos. Synthesize their recommendations with emphasis on minimizing moving parts while preserving feature richness.
close_reason: Spawned two independent planner sub-agents with clean prompts and no repo references. Both returned lean architecture recommendations emphasizing a single assistant interface, lightweight workflow runner, role prompts/configs, tool adapters/gateway, artifact/run ledger, and approval/policy gates while deferring department services, persistent agents, queues, plugin marketplace, vector/graph memory, and broad debate loops.

title: smoke: Beads adoption check
description: Verify that Beads is initialized, ready work is visible, the JSONL mirror exports, and preflight checks run in this repo.
acceptance_criteria: bd ready shows orch-9op; bd export writes .beads/issues.jsonl; bd stats runs; bd preflight --check runs; issue closes with this verification evidence.
close_reason: Adoption verified: bd ready showed orch-9op; bd export wrote .beads/issues.jsonl; bd stats ran; bd lint passed after adding acceptance criteria; bd stale/orphans passed; bd preflight --check executed but Go-specific checks are not applicable to this repo/toolchain.
